title: Stellar Wave 4 — Co-signing and multisig
goal: Make Compass a policy-gated co-signer for Stellar. Add a multi-signer-aware co-signing contract that generalizes the single-signer SignerAdapter (NOT typed on VersionedTransaction; operates on a base64 TransactionEnvelope XDR string), and a Stellar co-signer implementing Wave 0's ChainAdapter.cosign + inspectAccount. Compass holds its OWN testnet keypair (additional signer, never the user's key), adds ONLY its signature on the brain's ALLOW, binds the envelope to the evaluated candidate, reads real signers/weights/thresholds from Horizon, and never submits. On DENY/ESCALATE Compass does not sign, so the account threshold is unmet and the network rejects the transaction. The brain stays untouched and the Solana single-signer path stays green. Testnet-only.

openQuestions.question: Does Compass sign server-side with its own key, or return an unsigned-by-Compass envelope plus a signing intent for some external signer?
openQuestions.resolution: DECIDED: Compass signs with its OWN Stellar testnet key server-side, because Compass IS one of the account's required signers. cosign returns the envelope augmented with only Compass's signature; it never submits. Compass never holds or uses the user's key. The keypair is loaded only behind COMPASS_STELLAR_SIGNER_ENABLED=true and is testnet-only.

openQuestions.question: How is the account's multisig configured for the demo so that Compass's signature is required to meet the threshold?
openQuestions.resolution: DECIDED: Pre-set the account so the user's master weight plus Compass's signer key sum to the medium/high threshold, meaning neither alone meets it. Wave 4 only READS and verifies this via inspectAccount; the actual account setup (setOptions to add Compass as a signer and tune thresholds) is detailed and owned by Wave 6. Wave 4 depends on that configuration but does not perform it.

openQuestions.question: What exactly is hashed for the envelope<->candidate binding so Compass cannot be tricked into signing a different tx than the one it evaluated?
openQuestions.resolution: DECIDED: A SHA-256 over the canonical, deterministic representation of the envelope's operations plus source account and sequence (the signature-base material the brain evaluated in Waves 2-3). cosign recomputes this fingerprint from the supplied envelope and refuses (ENVELOPE_CANDIDATE_MISMATCH) unless it equals candidate.fingerprint. This closes the in-signer binding gap that the existing Solana flow leaves open.

acceptanceCriteria: Compass adds ONLY its own signature to the envelope; it never signs with or holds the user's key.
Compass never submits the transaction unilaterally; cosign returns the augmented XDR and stops.
cosign is gated on the brain's ALLOW; on DENY or unresolved REQUIRE_HUMAN_APPROVAL it returns POLICY_NOT_ALLOWED and adds no signature.
inspectAccount reads real signers, weights, and low/med/high thresholds from Horizon.
Envelope-to-candidate binding is enforced: the envelope fingerprint must equal candidate.fingerprint or cosign returns ENVELOPE_CANDIDATE_MISMATCH and signs nothing.
With Compass's signature absent, the account threshold is unmet, verified against the inspected account configuration.
The co-signing contract operates on a base64 TransactionEnvelope XDR string and is NOT typed on VersionedTransaction.
Compass's signer is testnet-only behind COMPASS_STELLAR_SIGNER_ENABLED; mainnet returns COMPASS_SIGNER_MAINNET_FORBIDDEN.
Missing Compass signer configuration returns COMPASS_SIGNER_NOT_CONFIGURED.
The secret seed never appears in results, audit metadata, or logs.
The brain (policy engine, LLM judge, sanitizer, COMPASS_DECISIONS, MCP proxy) is untouched and the Solana single-signer signing path stays green.
nonGoals: No production custody of any private key.
No mainnet support of any kind.
No submitting on the user's behalf by default (the user or the network submits).
No automated keeper or background execution engine.
No change to the brain (policy engine, LLM judge, sanitizer, COMPASS_DECISIONS, MCP proxy).
No change to any Solana provider or Solana signing behavior.
No multisig account setup (setOptions/threshold tuning) — that is owned by Wave 6.

tasks.title: Add RED tests for the Stellar co-signing contract
tasks.description: Write failing tests asserting the multi-signer-aware CosignAdapter contract shape: it operates on a base64 EnvelopeXdr string (not VersionedTransaction), exposes getAddress, cosign, and inspectAccount, and exports the CosignResult refusal union. Assert no legacy/ import appears in any new Wave 4 file.
testsSpec: A class implementing getAddress + cosign + inspectAccount satisfies CosignAdapter and compiles
cosign signature accepts (envelopeXdr: string, candidate, decision) and returns CosignResult
CosignResult refusal reasons include POLICY_NOT_ALLOWED, ENVELOPE_CANDIDATE_MISMATCH, COMPASS_SIGNER_NOT_CONFIGURED, COMPASS_SIGNER_MAINNET_FORBIDDEN
The contract does not import or reference @solana/web3.js VersionedTransaction
No legacy/ import appears in any new Wave 4 file

tasks.title: Implement stellarCosignerContracts.ts
tasks.description: Create the multi-signer-aware contract that generalizes the single-signer SignerAdapter: EnvelopeXdr (base64 string), CosignCandidate (candidateId + fingerprint), CosignConfig, CosignResult discriminated union, and the CosignAdapter interface. Reuse CompassDecision from shared contracts and AccountSignerState from Wave 0. Keep types separate from behavior; introduce no new decision types.

tasks.title: Add RED tests for cosign gating, binding, and testnet guard
tasks.description: Write failing tests for the Stellar co-signer: ALLOW adds only Compass's signature and preserves the user's, DENY/ESCALATE refuse with POLICY_NOT_ALLOWED, fingerprint mismatch refuses with ENVELOPE_CANDIDATE_MISMATCH, cosign never submits, env-gate and mainnet guard, and seed non-exposure.
testsSpec: cosign with decision ALLOW and matching fingerprint returns ok:true with exactly one added (Compass) signature, user signature preserved
cosign with decision DENY returns ok:false POLICY_NOT_ALLOWED and adds no signature
cosign with unresolved REQUIRE_HUMAN_APPROVAL returns POLICY_NOT_ALLOWED
cosign with a non-matching envelope fingerprint returns ENVELOPE_CANDIDATE_MISMATCH and signs nothing
cosign never invokes any submit/send path (no Horizon submit call)
mainnet network passphrase / Horizon target -> COMPASS_SIGNER_MAINNET_FORBIDDEN
the secret seed never appears in results, audit metadata, or logs

tasks.title: Implement stellarCosigner.ts (cosign + inspectAccount)
tasks.description: Create the Stellar co-signer implementing Wave 0's ChainAdapter.cosign and inspectAccount. cosign: env/network guard, decode envelope (Wave 2), compute fingerprint and bind to candidate, gate on ALLOW, add ONLY Compass's testnet signature, return augmented base64 XDR, never submit. inspectAccount: read signers/weights/low-med-high thresholds from Horizon (Wave 1) into AccountSignerState. Compass keypair behind COMPASS_STELLAR_SIGNER_ENABLED, testnet-only.

tasks.title: Add RED tests for inspectAccount multisig read
tasks.description: Write failing tests asserting inspectAccount maps Horizon signers/weights/thresholds into AccountSignerState and that, given the demo configuration, the threshold is unmet without Compass's weight.
testsSpec: inspectAccount maps Horizon signers (key + weight) into AccountSignerState
inspectAccount maps low_threshold / med_threshold / high_threshold into AccountSignerState thresholds
given user master weight + Compass weight summing to the threshold, removing Compass's weight leaves the threshold unmet
inspectAccount performs no signing and no submission

tasks.title: Validate Wave 4 and pause for review
tasks.description: Run the full validation suite and confirm the brain is untouched, the Solana single-signer path stays green, and everything is testnet-only before review.
